# Trellis AI guide

Last verified against Trellis main (2026-06-18).

Canonical full guide:
https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qlever-llc/trellis/main/docs/static/llms-full.txt

Use this file when generating code for a Trellis service repository or browser
app repository. Prefer the full guide when you need examples, contract workflow,
prepared events, browser-app wiring, generated SDK notes, or verification
commands.

## Current model

- Trellis runtime authority comes from deployment authority and identity
  authority.
- Deployment authority proposal/desired `needs` are grouped by `contracts`,
  `surfaces`, `capabilities`, and `resources`. Materialized authority is a
  projection; runtime `grants` are grouped by `capabilities`, `surfaces`, and
  `nats`, and permissions require current materialization for the accepted
  desired authority version. Stale or obsolete persisted projections are repaired
  by Trellis storage upgrade and reconciliation.
- The runtime `contracts` store is a manifest cache keyed by digest, not durable
  authority. If Trellis asks for a missing manifest or prunes an invalid cached
  manifest, present the full contract manifest again instead of editing runtime
  authority or implementation-offer records.
- Caller-visible communication is contract-driven and runs through generated
  RPC, event, feed, and operation surfaces. Resources, state, files, and jobs are
  contract-declared runtime surfaces; jobs are service-private background work.
- Normal TypeScript contract authoring imports `define*Contract(...)` helpers from
  the browser-safe `@qlever-llc/trellis` root. Runtime-specific helpers,
  generated SDKs, browser auth, device activation, telemetry, and advanced
  contract tooling stay on explicit subpaths.
- Durable event processing uses contract-declared `eventConsumers` resources;
  `eventConsumers.<group>.uses` selects dependency events by top-level `uses`
  alias, and `eventConsumers.<group>.self` selects events owned by the same
  contract. Dependency durable consumption remains authority-backed by top-level
  `uses`; runtime durable consumers are Trellis-provisioned only.
- Rust durable event listeners use descriptor-backed `listen_event(...)` from a
  connected service runtime and must not provide caller-owned durable names. The
  returned handle supports `abort()`; aborting a durable listener removes that
  handler registration so re-registering can resume delivery from the same
  Trellis-provisioned durable consumer.
- Trellis does not provide application dependency injection. Handler arguments
  contain only Trellis-owned runtime data. Application resources are supplied by
  normal JavaScript closure or factory patterns.
- Trellis libraries do not expose raw NATS connection handles as application API.
  Use generated surfaces, service resource handles, and `TrellisConnection` for
  lifecycle status and shutdown.
- Use operations for caller-visible async workflows. Use jobs for service-private
  background execution.
- For service-private work that must be singleton per logical entity, declare
  job `keyConcurrency` instead of building ad hoc scheduler dedupe. Defaults
  should fail clearly (`stalePolicy: "fail-stale"`, `whenFull: "reject"`);
  coalescing or replacing queued work must be explicit.
- Operations support declared typed errors, following the same error-declaration
  rules as RPC errors on the operation descriptor.
- Model expected public failures with declared errors or `Result`-style values.
- TypeScript services use `service.createSqlOutbox(...)` for SQL transactional
  event enqueue and job submission. Close over the outbox object as a plain dependency, use
  `outbox.transaction(...)` around all DB work and `event.*.*.enqueue(...)` or
  `job.*.*.create/submit(...)` calls that must commit atomically, and do not create local outbox wrappers or manual
  `SqlOutboxRepository` handler wiring. Normal event facades expose `prepare`,
  `publish`, and `listen`, not `enqueue`.
- Configure TypeScript SQL outbox with generic `executor` and `transaction`
  options. Drizzle remains optional through `@qlever-llc/trellis/service/drizzle`
  adapter helpers, but do not generate direct `createSqlOutbox({ drizzle })` sugar
  or per-handler Drizzle wrapping. Trellis owns helper-table migration artifacts
  from `getSqlOutboxMigrations(...)`; services run them with their normal
  migration tool.
- NATS KV outbox/inbox helpers remain non-transactional with arbitrary SQL or
  external side effects; do not use them to claim a database write and event
  enqueue committed atomically.
- TypeScript telemetry helpers are exported from `@qlever-llc/trellis/telemetry`.
  Use `recordTrellisDuration(...)` for low-cardinality OpenTelemetry duration
  histograms and `recordTrellisError(...)` for error counters; never put user IDs,
  session keys, flow IDs, request IDs, trace IDs, contract IDs, NATS subjects, or
  URLs in metric attributes.
- Generated TypeScript SDK event aliases are event bodies. Runtime event metadata
  is available through `PreparedTrellisEvent.header` and listener context.
- For live event assertions in Deno-first TypeScript integration tests, start
  `runtime.captureEvents(...)` before publishing and assert the decoded
  `{ event, payload, context, receivedAt }` records. Prefer this over raw NATS
  subscriptions or outbox-only assertions when the test must observe a published
  event through Trellis runtime authority.
- `@qlever-llc/trellis-test` exports assertion helpers including
  `assertEventCaptured`, `assertEventsCaptured`, `assertNoEventCaptured`,
  `assertNoEventDuring`, `assertJobCompleted`, `assertOperationCompleted`,
  `assertRpcOk`, `assertRpcEventuallyOk`, and `assertRpcErr` for Deno-first
  TypeScript tests. The captured-event helpers accept generated
  `runtime.captureEvents(...)` captures directly, and `assertJobCompleted`
  accepts generated service job refs directly.
- Event subject `params` must point at string, number, or integer-compatible
  payload fields. For `anyOf` or `oneOf` event payload schemas, every variant must
  contain each routed pointer.

## Service repo setup

- Keep Trellis-wide AI guidance in this file or `llms-full.txt`; keep local paths,
  commands, secrets, and domain rules in the service repo's own `AGENTS.md`.
- Put TypeScript service source next to a local `deno.json`, `deno.jsonc`, or
  `package.json`. Single-contract services normally expose a top-level
  `contract.ts`; multi-contract services use `contracts/*.ts`. Contract source
  files should default-export the `define*Contract(...)` result.
- Wrap TypeScript Trellis generation in `deno task prepare`.
- In TypeScript services, run prepare/generation after contract changes before
  typing extracted handlers. Import generated SDK aliases such as
  `OrdersCreateHandler` for extracted RPC/event/feed/operation/job
  handlers. Inline `service.handle...` handlers still infer at the registration
  site.
- TypeScript handlers may return source `defineError(...)` instances when their
  serialized data matches the declared generated SDK error data; generated SDK
  aliases type those returns structurally on the handler side.
- For Deno-first TypeScript service integration tests, prefer the public
  `@qlever-llc/trellis-test/integration` helpers and
  `/integration/runner`. External service repos provide
  `trellis.integration.ts` with an explicit `runtime.trellis.command`,
  `denoTestArgs`, and case descriptors. Tests use
  `trellisIntegrationTest(...)`, `runtimeScopeForCase(CASE_ID)`, case-scoped
  contract ids/subjects/names/resource keys, contract approval/provisioning,
  `TrellisService.connect(...)`, and `runtime.connectClient(...)`. Serial runs
  call the runner with `--config`; parallel runs add `--parallel --jobs <n>`,
  where `--jobs` maps to child `DENO_JOBS`, not a `deno test --jobs` flag. Keep
  one case per module and do not rely on cleanup or execution order.
- Test Trellis runtime behavior through live integration tests. Do not use fake
  NATS, fake Trellis runtimes, fake auth, fake generated clients, or hand-built
  bootstrap/control-plane responders to prove Trellis behavior. If a live test
  needs a missing failure or inspection surface, add the smallest named test
  helper instead of enriching a fake.
- Wrap Rust Trellis generation in `cargo xtask prepare`.
- Browser app repos should read the Svelte AI guide before wiring
  `createTrellisApp`, `TrellisProvider`, generated service SDK aliases for
  contract `uses`, Vite aliases, or `svelte-check` path maps. Do not generate or
  import an app SDK just to type a Svelte app client; use
  `TrellisClientFor<typeof contract>` from the app source contract.
- Browser apps should let Trellis helpers own auth redirects and treat plain
  callbacks after expired login as auth-restart opportunities, not errors.
- Browser apps that sign users out should use Trellis browser auth helpers for
  signed HTTP logout; do not call `Auth.Sessions.Logout` from the active app
  connection, hard-code IdP logout endpoints, or invent arbitrary post-logout
  return URLs.
- Record the local contract path, generation command, format command, typecheck
  command, test command, run command, and database migration command in the local
  `AGENTS.md`.

## Language guides

- TypeScript services:
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qlever-llc/trellis/main/docs/static/llms-typescript.txt
- Svelte browser apps:
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qlever-llc/trellis/main/docs/static/llms-svelte.txt
- Rust services:
  https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qlever-llc/trellis/main/docs/static/llms-rust.txt

## Useful links

- Full AI guide: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qlever-llc/trellis/main/docs/static/llms-full.txt
- TypeScript AI guide: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qlever-llc/trellis/main/docs/static/llms-typescript.txt
- Svelte AI guide: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qlever-llc/trellis/main/docs/static/llms-svelte.txt
- Rust AI guide: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qlever-llc/trellis/main/docs/static/llms-rust.txt
- TypeScript AGENTS template: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qlever-llc/trellis/main/docs/static/templates/trellis-service-typescript-AGENTS.md
- Rust AGENTS template: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qlever-llc/trellis/main/docs/static/templates/trellis-service-rust-AGENTS.md
- Combined AGENTS template: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/qlever-llc/trellis/main/docs/static/templates/trellis-service-AGENTS.md
- Docs-site path for the human guide: `/guides/ai/developing-trellis-services`
- Docs-site path for service integration testing: `/guides/testing-trellis-services`
- Docs-site path for the API reference: `/api`
